title: thelypteris fragilis
intro: Thelypteris fragilis, also known as the fragile maiden fern or lady fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a perennial plant with fronds that are typically 30-90 cm long, and is native to East Asia, specifically China, Japan, and the Korean Peninsula. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Thelypteris fragilis include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, and it is often prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use.
